content:
# Mark Chapter 14 — The EMET Bible (Literal)
**Translator:** EMET | **Source:** Codex Sinaiticus (Aleph, 01), folios 39v–40v (PDF pages 79–81) | **Source Image:** `source-texts/greek-NT/MRK_014_page_001.jpg`, `source-texts/greek-NT/MRK_014_page_002.jpg`, `source-texts/greek-NT/MRK_014_page_003.jpg`
**Secondary Witness:** Codex Vaticanus (B, 03) — consulted via [DigiVatLib](https://digi.vatlib.it/view/MSS_Vat.gr.1209)

---

¹ Now the Passover and the [Feast of] Unleavened-Bread was after two days. And the chief-priests and the scribes were-seeking how, having-seized him by deceit, they-might-kill [him].

² For they-were-saying, "Not during the feast, lest there-be an-uproar of-the people."

³ And he being in Bethany, in the house of Simon the leper, as-he-was-reclining, a-woman came having an-alabaster-jar of-ointment of-pure nard, very-costly. Having-broken the alabaster-jar, she-poured [it] over his head.

⁴ But some were-being-indignant among themselves: "For what has this waste of-the ointment happened?

⁵ For this ointment was-able to-be-sold for-more-than three-hundred denarii and to-be-given to-the poor." And they-were-scolding her.

⁶ But Jesus said, "Leave her-alone. Why do-you-cause trouble for-her? She-worked a-good work upon me.

⁷ For the poor you-have always with you, and whenever you-want you-are-able to-do good to-them; but me you-do not always have.

⁸ What she had, she-did. She-took-beforehand to-anoint my body for the burial.

⁹ Truly I-say to-you, wherever the gospel is-proclaimed in the whole world, also what this-[woman] did will-be-spoken for a-memorial of-her."

¹⁰ And Judas Iscariot, the one of the twelve, went-away to the chief-priests in-order-that he-might-deliver him to-them.

¹¹ And having-heard, they-rejoiced and promised to-give him money. And he-was-seeking how he-might conveniently deliver him.

¹² And on the first day of-Unleavened-Bread, when they-were-sacrificing the Passover, his disciples say to-him, "Where do-you-want [that] having-gone we-might-prepare in-order-that you-might-eat the Passover?"

¹³ And he-sends two of his disciples and says to-them, "Go into the city, and a-man carrying a-jar of-water will-meet you. Follow him,

¹⁴ and wherever he-enters, say to-the master-of-the-house, 'The Teacher says, Where is my guest-room where I-might-eat the Passover with my disciples?'

¹⁵ And he will-show you a-large upper-room, furnished [and] ready. And prepare for-us there."

¹⁶ And the disciples went-out and came into the city and found [it] just-as he-said to-them. And they-prepared the Passover.

¹⁷ And when-evening-came, he-comes with the twelve.

¹⁸ And as-they-were-reclining and eating, Jesus said, "Truly I-say to-you that one of-you will-deliver me — the-one eating with me."

¹⁹ They-began to-be-grieved and to-say to-him one by one, "Surely not I?"

²⁰ And he-said to-them, "One of-the twelve — the-one dipping with me into the dish.

²¹ Because the Son of Man goes just-as it-has-been-written concerning him, but woe to-that man through whom the Son of Man is-delivered! It-would-be good for-him if that man had not been-born."

²² And as-they-were-eating, having-taken bread, having-blessed, he-broke and gave to-them and said, "Take — this is my body."

²³ And having-taken a-cup, having-given-thanks, he-gave to-them, and they-all drank from it.

²⁴ And he-said to-them, "This is my blood of-the covenant, the-[blood] being-poured-out for many.

²⁵ Truly I-say to-you that I-will no-longer drink of the fruit of-the vine until that day when I-drink it new in the kingdom of God."

²⁶ And having-sung-a-hymn, they-went-out to the Mount of Olives.

²⁷ And Jesus says to-them, "All of-you will-be-scandalized, because it-has-been-written, 'I-will-strike the shepherd, and the sheep will-be-scattered.'

²⁸ But after I-am-raised, I-will-go-before you into Galilee."

²⁹ And Peter said to-him, "Even-if all will-be-scandalized, yet not I."

³⁰ And Jesus says to-him, "Truly I-say to-you that today — this night — before [the] rooster crows twice, you will-deny me three-times."

³¹ But he was-saying exceedingly, "Even-if it-is-necessary for-me to-die-with you, I-will not deny you." And likewise also they-were all saying.

³² And they-come to a-place whose name [is] Gethsemane. And he-says to-his disciples, "Sit here while I-pray."

³³ And he-takes-along Peter and James and John with him, and he-began to-be-deeply-distressed and troubled.

³⁴ And he-says to-them, "My soul is deeply-grieved to the-point-of death. Remain here and watch."

³⁵ And having-gone-forward a-little, he-fell on the ground and was-praying that if it-is possible, the hour might-pass from him.

³⁶ And he-was-saying, "Abba, Father, all-things [are] possible for-you. Take this cup from me. But not what I want, but what you [want]."

³⁷ And he-comes and finds them sleeping, and he-says to Peter, "Simon, are-you-sleeping? Were-you not strong-[enough] to-watch one hour?

³⁸ Watch and pray, in-order-that you-might not come into testing. The spirit [is] willing, but the flesh [is] weak."

³⁹ And again having-gone-away, he-prayed, saying the same word.

⁴⁰ And again having-come, he-found them sleeping, for their eyes were-being-weighed-down. And they-did not know what to-answer him.

⁴¹ And he-comes the third-[time] and says to-them, "Are-you-sleeping the remaining [time] and resting? It-is-enough. The hour has-come. Behold, the Son of Man is-being-delivered into the hands of-sinners.

⁴² Rise, let-us-go. Behold, the-one delivering me has-drawn-near."

⁴³ And immediately, while-he-was still speaking, Judas — one of the twelve — arrives, and with him a-crowd with swords and clubs, from the chief-priests and the scribes and the elders.

⁴⁴ And the-one delivering him had-given them a-signal, saying, "Whomever I-kiss, he is-[the one]. Seize him and lead [him] away securely."

⁴⁵ And having-come, immediately approaching him, he-says, "Rabbi!" And he-kissed him.

⁴⁶ And they laid the hands upon him and seized him.

⁴⁷ But a-certain-one of-those standing-by, having-drawn the sword, struck the servant of-the high-priest and cut-off his ear.

⁴⁸ And answering, Jesus said to-them, "As against a-robber you-came-out with swords and clubs to-take me?

⁴⁹ Daily I-was with you in the temple teaching, and you-did not seize me. But [this is] in-order-that the scriptures might-be-fulfilled."

⁵⁰ And having-left him, they-all fled.

⁵¹ And a-certain young-man was-following him, having-wrapped a-linen-cloth over [his] naked [body]. And they-seize him,

⁵² but he, having-left-behind the linen-cloth, fled naked.

⁵³ And they-led Jesus to the high-priest, and all the chief-priests and the elders and the scribes come-together.

⁵⁴ And Peter followed him from-afar, up-to inside the courtyard of-the high-priest. And he-was sitting-with the servants and warming-himself at the fire.

⁵⁵ And the chief-priests and the whole council were-seeking testimony against Jesus to-put him-to-death, and they-were not finding [any].

⁵⁶ For many were-bearing-false-witness against him, and the testimonies were not the-same.

⁵⁷ And some, having-stood-up, were-bearing-false-witness against him, saying,

⁵⁸ "We heard him saying, 'I will-destroy this temple made-with-hands, and after three days I-will-build another not-made-with-hands.'"

⁵⁹ And not-even so was their testimony the-same.

⁶⁰ And the high-priest, having-stood-up in [the] midst, asked Jesus, saying, "Do-you-answer nothing? What do-these testify-against you?"

⁶¹ But he-was-silent and answered nothing. Again the high-priest was-asking him and says to-him, "Are you the Christ, the Son of-the Blessed?"

⁶² And Jesus said, "I am. And you-will-see the Son of Man sitting at [the] right of the Power and coming with the clouds of-heaven."

⁶³ And the high-priest, having-torn his garments, says, "What further need do-we-have of-witnesses?

⁶⁴ You-heard the blasphemy. What does-it-seem to-you?" And they-all condemned him to-be deserving of-death.

⁶⁵ And some began to-spit on him and to-cover his face and to-strike him and to-say to-him, "Prophesy!" And the servants received him with slaps.

⁶⁶ And Peter being below in the courtyard, one of-the servant-girls of-the high-priest comes,

⁶⁷ and having-seen Peter warming-himself, having-looked-at him, she-says, "You also were with the Nazarene — Jesus."

⁶⁸ But he-denied, saying, "I-do not know nor understand what you are-saying." And he-went-out into the forecourt. And a-rooster crowed.

⁶⁹ And the servant-girl, having-seen him, began again to-say to-those standing-by, "This [man] is one of-them."

⁷⁰ But he was-denying again. And after a-little, again those standing-by were-saying to Peter, "Truly you-are of-them, for you-also are a-Galilean."

⁷¹ But he-began to-curse and to-swear, "I-do not know this man of-whom you-speak!"

⁷² And immediately a-rooster crowed a-second-time. And Peter remembered the word, how Jesus said to-him, "Before [the] rooster crows twice, you-will-deny me three-times." And having-thrown-himself-down, he-was-weeping.

---
## EMET's Textual Notes

1. **14:24 — "blood of the covenant":** Sinaiticus reads ΤΟ ΑΙΜΑ ΜΟΥ ΤΗΣ ΔΙΑΘΗΚΗΣ — "my blood of the covenant." Some manuscripts add "new" before "covenant" (harmonizing with Luke 22:20 and 1 Corinthians 11:25). Sinaiticus and Vaticanus lack "new."

2. **14:30 — "twice... three times":** Sinaiticus preserves the double cock-crow, unique to Mark. Matthew, Luke, and John have only one cock-crow. This detail is generally regarded as authentic — it is the harder, more specific reading.

3. **14:36 — "Abba, Father":** Mark preserves the Aramaic ΑΒΒΑ alongside the Greek ΠΑΤΗΡ. This bilingual address is one of the most intimate moments in the Gospels.

4. **14:51-52 — The naked young man:** This episode is unique to Mark. The identity of the young man remains one of the great mysteries of NT scholarship. Some identify him with Mark himself; others see a symbolic connection to the young man at the empty tomb (16:5).

5. **14:62 — "I am" (ΕΓΩ ΕΙΜΙ):** Sinaiticus preserves Jesus's direct affirmative response to the high priest. Matthew softens this to "You have said so" (26:64). Mark's ΕΓΩ ΕΙΜΙ is the most direct confession of messianic identity in the Synoptics.

6. **14:72 — "having thrown himself down" (ΕΠΙΒΑΛΩΝ):** This participle is notoriously difficult. It may mean "having thrown himself down," "having covered [his head]," or "having begun [to weep]." The exact meaning is uncertain; I render it as "having thrown himself down."

— EMET, AI Translator
